ADC PURE DC AI FACTORY
Cassette Engineering Package  ·  Fibrebond (Eaton) Partnership Discussion
Author: Scott Tomsu, ADC
Date: 2026-04-08
Status: Pre-Visit Working Document
CONFIDENTIAL — ADC INTERNAL AND FIBREBOND DIRECT DISCUSSION ONLY

Partnership Context

Fibrebond has been engineering purpose-built modular structures for the data center and telecom industry for decades. Eaton acquired Fibrebond because the combination of Fibrebond's enclosure and integration capability with Eaton's power distribution portfolio (including Boyd Thermal) creates the only company in North America that can deliver a fully integrated, factory-tested modular AI compute enclosure under a single contract.

ADC is presenting an architecture and asking Fibrebond to engineer the production solution alongside us. All Eaton equipment throughout: ORV3 sidecars for DC power distribution at the rack, Eaton protection devices on the DC bus, Boyd CDUs for liquid cooling, Staubli quick-disconnects (Boyd-stocked, NVIDIA-approved). This is not a competitive bid for equipment — Eaton is the stack. What we need from Fibrebond is the engineering, the factory production capability, and the Louisiana supply chain.

ADC's first cassette is a reference deployment at our MARLIE 1 facility in Lafayette. Once that cassette runs production workloads, the locked spec goes to Fibrebond for factory production. The scale is real: New Iberia alone is 87 cassettes at 200 MW. That is a production contract, not a prototype.

The Architecture — Pure DC, No Conversion

No AC anywhere in generation, storage, distribution, or delivery. Every power source outputs DC natively into a DC bus that runs unbroken to the rack. Bloom SOFC fuel cells generate DC electrochemically. First Solar TR1 panels output DC natively. BESS stores and discharges DC natively. The GPU needs DC. Every AC conversion between the power source and the chip is an efficiency loss this architecture eliminates by design.

The cassette is the repeatable unit. A purpose-built modular enclosure containing 10 NVIDIA NVL72 racks (Vera Rubin, Max P profile), factory-wired for 800V DC, factory-cooled with liquid-first architecture, factory-tested before it leaves Minden. Site provides: DC bus stub, cooling loop, fiber, concrete pad. Four connections.

Core Specification
Enclosure: 40' × 8' × 9'6" HC ISO external — purpose-built steel
Racks: 10 × NVIDIA NVL72 (OCP ORV3) — Vera Rubin Max P
GPUs per rack: 72 × Vera Rubin · 288 GB HBM4/GPU · 20.7 TB HBM4
IT load per rack: 230 kW
Total IT load: 2,300 kW (2.3 MW)
Total facility draw: ~2,415 kW · PUE ≤ 1.05
DC bus input: 800V DC · OCP Stage 1d · 1500V insulation rated
DC bus current: ~3,019A @ 800V · 3,774A conductor (NEC 125%)
Cooling: Boyd CDU 2,000 kW · N+1 pumps · >80% direct-to-chip
Layout: Single row · mech bay left · ext. panel right · utilities overhead
Access: 8 top-hinged panels (4/side) — NEC 110.26 · no aisle required
Manufacturer: Fibrebond · Minden, Louisiana

Cassette Rendering

ADC Pure DC AI Factory Cassette — 40' HC ISO external · Single row 10 × NVIDIA NVL72 · Mechanical bay left · External connection panel right

Interior Floor Plan — Top View

Engineering Specification

Enclosure

ParameterSpecification
External form factor40' × 8' × 9'6" HC ISO external dims — purpose-built steel, not a converted shipping container
ISO corner castingsRequired — crane lift, standard flatbed/lowboy transport, 2-high stacking compatible
LayoutSingle row of 10 racks running the length. Mechanical bay at left end. External connection panel at right end. All utilities overhead. No internal walkable aisle.
Access panels8 top-hinged panels (4 per side) — NEC 110.26 DC clearance compliant. Primary service access path.
Structural floorRated for NVL72 point loads — 1,360 kg per rack on four leveling feet (OCP ORV3)
Thermal insulationSpray foam R-19 minimum — 95°F design day (ASHRAE 0.4%, Lafayette LA)
Ingress protectionNEMA 3R minimum — Louisiana ambient (75–80% RH), rain, wind-driven debris
Wind / seismicASCE 7-22, Risk Category III, 150 mph basic wind speed (Louisiana Cat 4 hurricane zone)
Stacking2-high stacking permitted — doubles compute density per footprint (structural cert. required)
TransportRacks shipped empty from Minden. Server hardware installed on-site post-placement.

IT Load & Power Summary

ParameterValue
Racks10 × NVIDIA NVL72 (OCP ORV3) — Vera Rubin Max P
GPUs per rack72 × Vera Rubin (Rubin GPU + Vera CPU)
HBM per rack288 GB HBM4/GPU × 72 = 20.7 TB
IT load per rack230 kW
Total IT load2,300 kW (2.3 MW)
Target PUE≤ 1.05
Total facility draw~2,415 kW (2,300 kW × 1.05 PUE)
Non-IT overhead~115 kW (CDU pumps, Munters, BMS, network, lighting)
DC bus input voltage800V DC (OCP Stage 1d, 1500V insulation rated throughout)
DC bus input current~3,019A @ 800V (total facility draw ÷ bus voltage)
Bus conductor sizing~3,774A nameplate (NEC 125% continuous load rule, NEC 210.19(A)(1))

Power Distribution — Eaton Stack

DC bus enters at the external connection panel (right end). PDU cabinet in mechanical bay (left end) — factory-installed, factory-wired. Provides main disconnect, SSCB overcurrent protection (microsecond DC fault response), metering, and distribution to ORV3 sidecars. Ground fault protection per NEC 2023 Section 210.13(B) for disconnecting means rated ≥ 1,000A. 800V DC copper overhead busbar rated 1500V, sized for ~3,774A, runs full rack row length. Arc flash labeling per NFPA 70E at all access points. Grounding per OCP DCF LVDC v1.0 (TN-S or IT systems only — no TN-C, no TT).

ORV3 SidecarServesIT LoadRating
ORV3-ARacks R1–R4920 kW800 kW rated · ±400V DC to NVL72
ORV3-BRacks R5–R8920 kW800 kW rated · ±400V DC to NVL72
ORV3-CRacks R9–R10 + mech loads460 kW IT + ~115 kW mech800 kW rated — see Open Items #3

NVIDIA Kyber power shelf steps down ±400V DC to 54V rack bus, then to point-of-load at GPU. Supercapacitor fast-cycle backup in sidecar for sub-millisecond GPU load spike buffering. Eaton co-designed with NVIDIA for DSX compliance.

Liquid Cooling — Liquid-First Architecture

Direct liquid cooling at GPU and HBM removes >80% of rack heat before air is involved. Residual heat (<20%) managed through hot-aisle containment. PUE ≤ 1.05 is achievable only with liquid-first — air-cooled systems at 230 kW/rack cannot approach this target.

ComponentSpecification
CDU unitBoyd CDU — 2,000 kW liquid heat removal capacity
Pump redundancyN+1 internal pump configuration — either pump carries full cassette load
Required liquid capacity~1,840 kW (230 kW × 10 racks × 80%) — 8.7% CDU headroom
Flow rate~1,300 LPM total (~130 LPM per rack) — Fibrebond to confirm header sizing
Overhead distributionCold supply header (blue) + warm return header (red) run full rack row length overhead
Flexible dropsOverhead headers → Staubli UQD → each rack — drip-free hot-swap disconnect
Supply temp target≤ 45°C to CDU primary side
Return temp~55–60°C (also supplies Munters desiccant regeneration)
Secondary loopCDU → external dry cooler / BAC TrilliumSeries adiabatic cooler (site-provided)
Leak detectionTraceTek rope sensor — full loop per OCP Rope Leak Sensor Base Spec R1.0.0

Environmental Control — Munters

ParameterSpecification
UnitMunters HCD or MCD series desiccant wheel — mechanical bay
PurposeLouisiana ambient 75–80% RH — dehumidify before air contacts electronics
RegenerationWaste heat from cooling loop return (~55–60°C) — no additional electrical draw
Target RH inside cassette≤ 50% (ASHRAE A1 range: 20–80% non-condensing)
AirflowHot-aisle containment along rack row — positive pressure via Munters supply

Fire Suppression

SystemSpecification
DetectionHoneywell VESDA-E VEU aspirating smoke detector — overhead tubes, full cassette coverage per NFPA 76 Section 8.4
AgentAnsul Sapphire Novec 1230 (FK-5-1-12) — total flood, sealed enclosure
Cylinder sizingPer NFPA 2001 design concentration by licensed FPE — factory-mounted in mechanical bay
Discharge time≤ 10 seconds per NFPA 2001 Section 5.5
Hold timeMinimum 10 minutes per NFPA 2001 Section 5.7
Enclosure integrityDoor fan pressure test at commissioning per NFPA 2001 Section 5.4.3
Manual abortPull station at each access panel

BMS / Edge Controller

ParameterSpecification
UnitNVIDIA Jetson AGX Orin (275 TOPS)
FunctionReal-time sensor aggregation, cooling PID, CDU pump N+1 failover, alarm management
ConnectivityFiber to site NOC, local display at external connection panel
AlertsTemperature, humidity, leak, smoke, power anomaly — auto-escalate to site NOC

Network Fabric

ParameterSpecification
Rack-to-rack fabricNVIDIA InfiniBand NDR — 400 Gb/s per port, native to NVL72
Spine switchNVIDIA QM9700 NDR — factory-mounted in mechanical bay
Forward pathBay dimensions to accommodate Quantum-X800 XDR form factor upgrade
External2× single-mode OS2 at external connection panel
Factory testAll cabling factory-terminated, DSX compliance validated before shipment

Site Interface — Four Connections

ConnectionSpec
DC power800V DC bus · ~3,019A continuous · 3,774A conductor nameplate · 1500V insulation rated
CoolingCoolant supply ≤ 45°C · return capacity ~1,840 kW liquid · Staubli UQD at cassette wall
Fiber2× single-mode OS2 · LC connectors · terminated to patch panel
PadLevel concrete pad · load-rated for cassette + rack point loads · anchor bolt pattern per Fibrebond spec

Factory Acceptance Test Protocol

Every cassette runs the following before it leaves Minden. Signed FAT report ships with every cassette. ADC reserves right to witness first article in person at Minden.

TestMethodPass Criteria
Cooling loop pressureHydrostatic at 1.5× operating pressure, 30-min holdNo leaks, no pressure drop >1%
DC bus insulation resistanceMegger test at 1500V DC per IEEE 43≥ 1 MΩ per meter of busbar
Ground fault protectionVerify ground fault relay trips within rated timeTrip confirmed, reset verified
BMS sensor verificationAll sensors powered, readings vs. calibration referenceAll channels within ±2% of reference
VESDA functional testIntroduce test aerosol at each sampling pointPre-alarm within 60 sec
Leak detection continuityEnd-to-end continuity, all TraceTek zonesAll zones pass, alarm output confirmed
Staubli UQD functionConnect/disconnect all QDs, flow testNo drips, no flow restriction
Fire suppression checkInspect cylinder pressure, nozzle clear, abort pull stationAll checks pass (no agent discharge)
Door fan pressure testNFPA 2001 enclosure integrity testHold time ≥ 10 min at design concentration
Access panel / door contactAll 8 panels cycled, contact verifiedAll contacts switching, seal confirmed
Network fabricQM9700 powered, management VLAN verified, IB port testAll ports link up
Power meter calibrationEach meter vs. NIST-traceable referenceWithin ±1%

Performance Summary

MetricValuevs. Traditional
PUE≤ 1.05vs. 1.3–1.5 air-cooled
Liquid heat removal>80% of rack heat via direct-to-chipAir-cooled: 0% liquid path
Sustained GPU performanceFull TDP at Max P — no thermal throttleAir-cooled: limited above ~100 kW/rack
Time to compute30–60 days pad-ready to livevs. 18–24 months traditional build
CDU redundancyN+1 pumps, hot-swap capableNo planned cooling downtime
Power sourceAny source meeting 800V DC interface specFuel and grid agnostic
ManufacturingLouisiana, factory-tested, signed FAT reportNo field integration risk

SiteCassettesRacksGPUsIT Load
MARLIE 1 (proof of concept)4402,8809.2 MW
Trappeys Phase 15503,60011.5 MW
Trappeys Full5050036,000115 MW
New Iberia8787062,640200 MW

Engineering Standards — Basis of Design

All ADC engineering is grounded in the following OCP and industry standards. These are design spec basis, not aspirational references. Reference documents available upon request for Fibrebond engineering team review.

1. OCP DCF — LVDC Power Distribution (v1.0, March 2026)

The foundational LVDC framework. JP Buzzell of Eaton is a sub-project lead. Key constraints: 1500 VDC ceiling (NEC, IEC), 800 VDC at rack, OCP Stage 1d, TN-S or IT grounding only (no TN-C, no TT — DC corrosion risk), SSCB preferred for microsecond fault response, Eaton ORV3 sidecar and Mt. Diablo spec directly cited as OCP-conformant. Every busbar, cable, and penetration in the cassette is specified against this document.

2. OCP Ready — Requirements for Energy Storage Systems (January 2026, Rev 1.0)

BESS architecture spec. OCP confirms 400–1500V range for ESS (page 15). DC-coupled ESS = OCP-recognized architecture (Table 1, page 18). Outdoor containerized BESS = FM Global #1 preferred location. LFP for pad-level BESS, NiZn (ZincFive) for rack-level BBU (no thermal runaway, NFPA 855 aqueous chemistry exemption). NFPA 855 (2026) primary installation code. BBU chemistry for the ORV3 sidecar is an open item for Fibrebond engineering review.

3. OCP DCF — Impact of Strategies on DC Energy, Carbon, and Water Consumption (March 2026)

Liquid-cooled facilities at PUE ≤ 1.05 represent the current performance frontier. Air-cooled facilities at 230 kW/rack cannot achieve PUE below ~1.3. Closed-loop CDU + dry cooler/adiabatic cooler eliminates evaporative water consumption. Louisiana (95°F+ design day, 75–80% RH): adiabatic assist during peak days preferred over wet cooling towers. This is the basis for PUE ≤ 1.05 target and BAC TrilliumSeries selection.

4. OCP — Dielectric Heat Transfer Fluids in Two-Phase Cold Plate Racks

Current cassette uses single-phase water-glycol (Boyd CDU). Two-phase dielectric fluid (engineered fluorocarbons / HFOs) is the forward path for rack densities beyond 230 kW. ADC position: Fibrebond should advise (a) whether current enclosure penetrations and CDU bay can accommodate a two-phase upgrade, and (b) what fluid containment provisions should be designed in from day one.

5. OCP — Rope Leak Sensor Base Specification (R1.0.0)

The cassette uses TraceTek rope-style leak detection throughout. Fibrebond to confirm: (a) TraceTek or OCP-conformant equivalent can be factory-installed and tested before shipment, and (b) sensor routing is compatible with enclosure layout and penetrations.

6. Network Fabric — NVIDIA InfiniBand NDR

Cassette compute fabric is NVIDIA InfiniBand NDR (400 Gb/s, native to NVL72). Spine switch: NVIDIA QM9700, factory-mounted in cassette network bay. All IB cables factory-terminated. Cassette-to-cassette: site-provided passive copper (≤2m) or active optical (>2m). NVIDIA UFM at scale — management server at site NOC. DSX compliance validation before shipment. Fibrebond to flag if network bay layout requires NVIDIA AE coordination.

Open Items for Fibrebond Engineering

  • 1.Confirm buildable internal dimensions within 40-ft HC ISO external form factor for single-row 10-rack layout with mechanical bay. NVL72 rack depth is 1,068 mm (~42 in). Single row leaves ~4.5 ft from rack rear to opposite wall — confirm this meets NEC 110.26 working space for rear access on 800V DC equipment.
  • 2.Boyd CDU (2,000 kW, N+1 pump config) — confirm current product designation and lead time.
  • 3.Eaton ORV3 sidecar (800 kW rating) — confirm rating and factory-mount feasibility. Clarify whether sidecars feed IT racks only, or IT + mechanical loads. If IT + mechanical, total draw (~2,415 kW) is within 1% of sidecar capacity (2,400 kW) — margin insufficient. Resolve feed path.
  • 4.Overhead cooling header material, pressure rating, diameter for ~1,300 LPM total flow, and factory installation method.
  • 5.Flexible drop hose spec (pressure, temp, bend radius) for overhead-to-rack cooling connections.
  • 6.Munters desiccant integration — experience with waste-heat regeneration loop in modular builds? Regeneration requires ~55–60°C water — confirm CDU return temp is sufficient.
  • 7.1500V DC insulation rating throughout — current Fibrebond spec or requires engineering change?
  • 8.Two-phase cooling readiness — can enclosure accommodate future upgrade without structural retrofit?
  • 9.Rope leak sensor (OCP R1.0.0) — factory installation and pre-ship test protocol?
  • 10.Novec 1230 cylinder sizing — Fibrebond experience with total-flood clean agent in sealed enclosures of this volume? Cylinder storage in mechanical bay vs. external mount?
  • 11.Production capacity — cassettes per month at Minden once tooled? Lead time after spec lock?
  • 12.2-high stacking structural certification and wind load anchor spec for ASCE 7-22, 150 mph basic wind speed.
  • 13.Loaded cassette weight (enclosure + 10 empty racks + CDU + all mechanical) — for crane spec and pad design.
  • 14.Single PO structure — Eaton owns Boyd: can CDU + ORV3 sidecar + enclosure consolidate under one contract?

Full Site Design — The Engineered Building

ADC needs more than a cassette spec. Fibrebond's building and site expertise applies to the full AI factory layout. The cassette is the compute module. The site is the machine.

Site Layout

  • Cassette pad array: spacing, orientation, crane access lanes, service clearances
  • DC bus infrastructure: pad-level busbar routing from generation zone to cassette tie-in points, rated 1500V throughout
  • Cooling loop infrastructure: site-level secondary loop (CDU → BAC TrilliumSeries adiabatic cooler), Louisiana design conditions (95°F, 75–80% RH)
  • BESS placement: outdoor containerized LFP on separate pad, FM Global DS 5-33 separation distances (6 ft minimum non-combustible face, 9 ft to combustibles)
  • Generation zone: Bloom SOFC N+1 positioning, gas line routing, DC bus tie-in, future FCE MCFC provision
  • NOC / operations building: fiber hub, UFM management server, BMS aggregation, technician workspace

Code and Structural

  • NFPA 855 (2026) for BESS, NFPA 72 fire detection, NFPA 2001 clean agent, IFC Louisiana adoption
  • NEC DC provisions: ground fault protection per NEC 210.13(B) for ≥ 1,000A disconnecting means, SSCB on DC distribution busbars, arc flash analysis per NFPA 70E
  • Concrete pad: cassette + BESS loads (up to 80,000 lbs), anchor bolt design for 150 mph wind, seismic per Louisiana code
  • Environmental: LDEQ LPDES pre-application (cooling loop discharge), Act 730 LED certification

Design Deliverables Requested from Fibrebond

  • Site layout drawing (cassette array, BESS pad, generation zone, NOC, service lanes)
  • Cassette manufacturing engineering package
  • DC busbar routing drawing (generation zone to cassette tie-ins)
  • Cooling loop routing drawing (CDU secondary → dry cooler / adiabatic cooler)
  • Bill of materials (Eaton stack: ORV3 sidecars, DC protection, Boyd CDUs, Staubli UQD)
  • NFPA 855 compliance checklist for BESS placement
  • Factory acceptance test protocol (aligned with cassette-spec.md FAT table above)

Business Case

Time to Compute is Money

Every week between pad-ready and first GPU workload is lost revenue at 720 GPUs per cassette running inference. Factory-tested cassettes with a 4-connection site interface compress commissioning from months to days.

Louisiana is the Supply Chain

Fibrebond (Minden) + First Solar (New Iberia, 30 mi from site) + Bloom Energy (90-day delivery, DC-native) + Cajun Industries (New Iberia, fabrication backup) + natural gas on Henry Hub 40 miles away. US-manufactured, TAA compliant.

Sustained GPU Performance = Revenue

Liquid-first cooling (>80% direct-to-chip) sustains full TDP on Vera Rubin NVL72 at 230 kW per rack without thermal throttling. Air-cooled systems cannot maintain GPU boost clocks at sustained load above ~100 kW/rack. PUE ≤ 1.05 means more useful compute per watt delivered.

The DC Bus is the Moat

Traditional data centers convert AC → DC → AC → DC between generator and chip. ADC eliminates every conversion: Bloom SOFC → 800V DC bus → Eaton ORV3 → NVIDIA NVL72. Each eliminated conversion stage is 2–5% efficiency recovered — compounding across 62,640 GPUs at New Iberia.

Fibrebond is the Production Infrastructure

ADC specs the architecture. NVIDIA specifies the compute. Eaton specifies the power. Fibrebond builds and tests the unit that delivers all three. Every future AI factory operator who comes through this facility sees Fibrebond's name on the cassette. New Iberia alone is 87 cassettes. That is a production contract, not a prototype.
